perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall (i) use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information and materials, whether oral, written or in any form whatsoever, of the other that may be reasonably understood, from legend s, the nature of such information itself and/or the circumstances of such information’s disclosure, to be confidential and/or proprietary thereto or to third parties to which either of them owes a duty of nondisclosure (collectively, “Confidential Informat ion”); (ii) take reasonable action in connection therewith, including without limitation at least the action that each takes to protect the confidentiality of its comparable proprietary assets; (iii) to the extent within their respective possession and/or control, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, immediately return to the provider thereof all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used or enjoyed after termination or expiration hereof, and (iv) with respect to any perso n to which disclosure is contemplated, require such person to execute an agreement providing for the treatment of Confidential Information set forth in clauses (i) through (iii). The foregoing shall not require separate written agreements with employees a nd agents already subject to written agreements substantially conforming to the requirements of this Section nor with legal counsel, certified public accountants, or other professional advisers under a professional obligation to maintain the confidences of clients.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall (i) use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information and materials, whether oral, written or in any form whatsoever, of the other that may be reasonably understood, from legends, the nature of such information itself and/or the circumstances of such information’s disclosure, to be confidential and/or proprietary thereto or to third parties to which either of them owes a duty of nondisclosure (collectively, “Confidential Information”); (ii) take reasonable action in connection therewith, including without limitation at least the action that each takes to protect the confidentiality of its comparable proprietary assets; (iii) to the extent within their respective possession and/or control, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, immediately return to the provider thereof all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used or enjoyed after termination or expiration hereof, and (iv) with respect to any person to which disclosure is contemplated, require such person to execute an agreement providing for the treatment of Confidential Information set forth in clauses (i) through (iii). The foregoing shall require separate written agreements with employees and agents already subject to written agreements substantially conforming to the requirements of this Section nor with legal counsel, certified public accountants, or other professional advisers under a professional obligation to maintain the confidences of clients.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not require' to 'shall require', this creates an in-text contradiction. The section now first indicates reasonable effort to maintain confidentially, but then enforces separate written agreements to employees/agents. This change contradicts the following sentence, that indicates that employees and agents with an already written agreement with conforming requirements should not be required to write other agreement. This creates confusion about whether additional agreements are necessary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall (i) use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information and materials, whether oral, written or in any form whatsoever, of the other that may be reasonably understood, from legend s, the nature of such information itself and/or the circumstances of such information’s disclosure, to be confidential and/or proprietary thereto or to third parties to which either of them owes a duty of nondisclosure (collectively, “Confidential Informat ion”); (ii) take reasonable action in connection therewith, including without limitation at least the action that each takes to protect the confidentiality of its comparable proprietary assets; (iii) to the extent within their respective possession and/or control, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, immediately return to the provider thereof all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used or enjoyed after termination or expiration hereof, and (iv) with respect to any perso n to which disclosure is contemplated, require such person to execute an agreement providing for the treatment of Confidential Information set forth in clauses (i) through (iii). The foregoing shall require separate written agreements with employees a nd agents already subject to written agreements substantially conforming to the requirements of this Section nor with legal counsel, certified public accountants, or other professional advisers under a professional obligation to maintain the confidences of clients.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'shall not require' to 'shall require' regarding separate written agreements with employees and agents, directly contradicting the intention of the original text. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, however, the change from "shall not require" to "shall require" regarding the need for separate written agreements with employees and agents already subject to written agreements substantially conforming to the requirements of this section introduces a direct conflict. This creates an inconsistency within the document, making it unclear whether additional agreements are necessary or not, thus resulting in a strong in-text contradiction as it is referencing itself.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligation of a person to protect the confidentiality of any information or materials shall terminate as to any information or materials which: (i) are, or become, public knowledge thr ough no act or failure to act of such person; (ii) are publicly disclosed by the proprietor thereof; (iii) are lawfully obtained without obligations of confidentiality by such person from a third party after reasonable inquiry regarding the authority of su ch third party to possess and divulge the same; (iv) are independently developed by such person from sources or through persons that such person can demonstrate had no access to Confidential Information; or (v) are lawfully known by such person at the time of disclosure other than by reason of discussions with or disclosures by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligation of a person to protect the confidentiality of any information or materials shall terminate as to any information or materials which: (i) are, or become, public knowledge through no act or failure to act of such person; (ii) are publicly disclosed by the proprietor thereof; (iii) are lawfully obtained without obligations of confidentiality by such person from a third party, regardless of inquiry regarding the authority of such third party to possess and divulge the same; (iv) are independently developed by such person from sources or through persons that such person can demonstrate had no access to Confidential Information; or (v) are lawfully known by such person at the time of disclosure other than by reason of discussions with or disclosures by the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'reasonable inquiry' clause in (iii), the obligation of confidentiality is undermined. Now, if information is obtained from a third party, there is no need to investigate whether the third party is authorized to divulge this information. This creates a contradiction, undermining the initial Confidentiality agreement. It introduces an in-text contradiction because it conflicts with the overall intent of the agreement to protect confidential information through "reasonable efforts".
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall (i) use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information and materials, whether oral, written or in any form whatsoever, of the other that may be reasonably understood, from legend s, the nature of such information itself and/or the circumstances of such information’s disclosure, to be confidential and/or proprietary thereto or to third parties to which either of them owes a duty of nondisclosure (collectively, “Confidential Informat ion”); (ii) take reasonable action in connection therewith, including without limitation at least the action that each takes to protect the confidentiality of its comparable proprietary assets; (iii) to the extent within their respective possession and/or control, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, immediately return to the provider thereof all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used or enjoyed after termination or expiration hereof, and (iv) with respect to any perso n to which disclosure is contemplated, require such person to execute an agreement providing for the treatment of Confidential Information set forth in clauses (i) through (iii). The foregoing shall not require separate written agreements with employees a nd agents already subject to written agreements substantially conforming to the requirements of this Section nor with legal counsel, certified public accountants, or other professional advisers under a professional obligation to maintain the confidences of clients.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'reasonable inquiry' clause, which directly contradicts the contradicted text's requirement that 'Parties shall use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality'. Removing the inquiry obligation means parties are no longer obligated to use 'reasonable efforts' in all cases, creating a direct conflict and weakening the confidentiality agreement. The Perturbation explanation emphasizes the fact that it 'undermines the initial Confidentiality agreement'. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferr ing any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall us e the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship. 2 The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding for a period of two (2) y ears from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall use the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship, except for marketing purposes. The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding indefinitely from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing' and 'except for marketing purposes' and 'indefinitely', this section now contradicts the specific return and use limitations, and time limitations of the confidential information. It introduces in-text contradiction because now parties can agree to not return or destroy the information, which goes against the disclosure. Parties can also use the confidential information for marketing, creating a contradiction within the business agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall use the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship, except for marketing purposes. The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding indefinitely from the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing', 'except for marketing purposes', and changes the time period to 'indefinitely'. This directly contradicts the original agreement by allowing parties to deviate from the original terms of returning/destroying confidential information, using it only for the Business Relationship, and the two-year limitation. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, which highlights how the additions create a conflict with the initially stated limitations.

perturbation.original_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the breaching Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will be entitled to recover from the other Party all costs incurred thereby, including without limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.
perturbation.changed_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non-breaching Party may seek monetary damages only, as the Parties agree that such damages are an adequate remedy. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, each Party shall bear its own costs, including attorney's fees, regardless of the outcome.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the text to 'seek monetary damages only', this contradicts the prior clause which states that there's no adequate remedy at law, creating a contradiction. Additionally, stating that 'each Party shall bear its own costs' contradicts the initial clause that states 'it will be entitled to recover from the other Party all costs incurred thereby, including without limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non-breaching Party may seek monetary damages only, as the Parties agree that such damages are an adequate remedy. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, each Party shall bear its own costs, including attorney's fees, regardless of the outcome.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and, consequently, itself in the 'Contradicted Text'. Initially, it acknowledges no adequate legal remedy and allows for injunctive relief and cost recovery. However, the changed text limits remedies to monetary damages and states that each party bears its own costs, directly conflicting with the idea of 'no adequate remedy at law' and the ability to recover costs. This is a clear inconsistency as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party makes any representation or warranty with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it, nor shall either Party or any of their respective representatives have any liability hereunder with respect to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information or the use thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party warrants that all Confidential Information disclosed by it is accurate and complete to the best of its knowledge and shall be liable for any damages resulting from inaccuracies or omissions, to the extent such inaccuracies or omissions are the result of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by directly opposing the original statement that 'Neither Party makes any representation or warranty'. Now, 'Each Party warrants that all Confidential Information disclosed by it is accurate and complete'. Also the phrase indicating, 'nor shall either Party or any of their respective representatives have any liability hereunder', is now being contracted since the changed text indicates that liability will be present due to inaccuracies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party warrants that all Confidential Information disclosed by it is accurate and complete to the best of its knowledge and shall be liable for any damages resulting from inaccuracies or omissions, to the extent such inaccuracies or omissions are the result of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and by extension, the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text. The original text states that neither party makes any warranty, while the changed text states that each party warrants the accuracy and completeness of the confidential information. The original text also denies liability, while the changed text asserts liability for damages resulting from inaccuracies. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since the changed section creates conflicting obligations regarding warranties and liability.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified only by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified by oral agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing this phrase to indicate that it may be modified by 'oral agreement between the Parties' directly contradicts the original clause of 'only by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties.' Since, both, a written or oral agreement can be used to modify this agreement, it causes an in-text contradiction, since it can be interpretted that both agreements have equal power to change the content of the initial agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be amended or modified by oral agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement can be modified by 'oral agreement between the Parties', which directly contradicts the original text stating modification can 'only' occur 'by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as both methods are now presented as valid, conflicting with each other and introducing ambiguity about which method should be followed. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text and causes a contradiction between the original text. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
